copyrightBoundary: Use short excerpts and project-specific analysis; do not archive or rewrite source articles.

sources.title: mattpocock/dictionary-of-ai-coding README
sources.excerpt: The upstream project presents AI coding jargon as learnable vocabulary and publishes a generated dictionary-style README.
sources.notes: Used for structure and source attribution only; this post does not reproduce entry text.

sources.title: mattpocock/dictionary-of-ai-coding internal/Curriculum.md
sources.excerpt: The curriculum groups terms into model, sessions, tools, failure modes, handoffs, memory and steering, and work patterns.
sources.notes: Used to translate the taxonomy at a section level.

sources.title: mattpocock/dictionary-of-ai-coding package.json
sources.excerpt: The package metadata marks the project private and does not declare a package license field.
sources.notes: Checked with the repository tree; no LICENSE file was present at the reviewed commit.

sources.title: Harness Hub docs/source-projects.md
sources.excerpt: Harness Hub records dictionary-of-ai-coding as reference-only because the reviewed snapshot had no license evidence.
sources.notes: Local policy source for copyright and redistribution boundary.

sources.title: Harness Hub docs/harness-vocabulary.md
sources.excerpt: Harness Hub keeps local-original vocabulary definitions and names the upstream dictionary as structural inspiration only.
sources.notes: Local term source for model, agent, harness, context, skill, tool, review, and validation boundaries.

sources.title: Harness Hub effective-interact interaction patterns
sources.excerpt: The research-explainer guidance says Harness Hub vocabulary explainers should use local definitions and avoid copying external glossary text.
sources.notes: Local rendering and explanation pattern source.

sources.title: Harness Hub docs/skill-quality-guide.md
sources.excerpt: The quality guide separates vocabulary boundary fixes from imported skill style rewrites and forbids copying external glossary text into local skills.

sources.title: Harness Hub docs/source-post-publishing.md
sources.excerpt: Source-post publishing turns source material into a source-backed project post and validates fact-inference separation before Pages deployment.
sources.notes: Publishing workflow source for this post.

sources.title: Harness Hub commit 80729a1
sources.excerpt: The commit adds harness vocabulary boundaries, source registry updates, effective-interact fixture coverage, and related tests.

sourceClaims.statement: 上游把 AI coding 的混乱归因于术语不透明，目标是用白话解释基础词，而不是写产品文档。
上游术语按七组组织：模型、会话与上下文、工具与环境、失败模式、交接、记忆与引导、工作模式。
所审上游快照没有 LICENSE 和 package license；无额外授权前，不应公开全文翻译。
Harness Hub 已将该仓库记为 reference-only，禁止复制条目正文、再分发内容或安装成 skill。
Harness Hub 已把外部术语启发改写成本地原创边界，覆盖 model、agent、harness、context、skill、tool、check、review 等词。
effective-interact 要求词汇解释使用本地定义、相邻概念对比和来源引用，且不复制外部 glossary 文本。
skill quality guide 把 glossary 源当作路由和语义参考，而不是改写 imported skill 或复制定义的理由。
source-post publishing 适合承载本文：来源、claims、观点、映射、验证在同一链路。
此前 dictionary-driven 迭代不是导入内容，而是新增本地词汇文档、解释器支持、来源策略和测试。

viewpoints.statement: 本文不做全文翻译，而是给读者三件事：原仓库结构导读、本地变更清单、以及这些变更是否有效的反思。
原仓库的核心价值是 7 层组织：模型、会话、工具、失败模式、交接、记忆与引导、工作模式；它让读者先建立概念地图，再看具体术语。
本地 vocabulary 迭代有效，因为它把 model、agent、harness、skill、tool、check、review 的边界写成 Harness Hub 自己的工程语言。
effective-interact 的相关改造部分有效：结构化解释和验证有价值，但如果文章首屏被组件、边界和来源声明占满，就会偏离读者问题。
source registry 与 quality guide 的变更有效且不冗余：前者限定授权和使用方式，后者防止为了术语统一去重写 imported skill bodies。
仍需优化的是表达层：公开 blog 应固定为原仓库导读、本地变更、有效性反思，而不是 source-ledger 式审计报告。
下一步不应扩成长期双语 glossary 或 CMS；除非上游授权或本地使用频率变化，否则保留边界文档和验证链路即可。
